content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing a video game, and is now fighting the final boss.

The boss has HHH health points. Each attack of Chef reduces the health of the boss by XXX.

Chef also has a special attack that can be used **at most once**, and will decrease the health of the boss by YYY.

Chef wins when the health of the boss is ≤0\leq 0≤0.

What is the **minimum** number of attacks needed by Chef to win?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case will contain three space-separated integers H,X,YH, X, YH,X,Y — the parameters described in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of attacks needed by Chef to win.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef can attack the boss 444 times normally. This results in 25+25+25+25=10025 + 25 + 25 + 25 = 10025+25+25+25=100 damage, which is enough to defeat the boss.
Test case 222: Chef can attack the boss 222 times normally, then use the special attack. This results in 29+29+45=10329 + 29 + 45 = 10329+29+45=103 damage, which is enough to defeat the boss.
Test case 333: Chef can proceed as follows:

First, use the special attack. This leaves the boss with 46−2=4446 - 2 = 4446−2=44 health.
Then, use 444444 normal attacks to defeat the boss, since each one does 111 damage.

This takes a total of 44+1=4544 + 1 = 4544+1=45 attacks.
Test case 444: Chef can use the special attack to immediately bring the health of the boss to zero, hence only needing one attack.
